Add all 9 utility tools to apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts so they appear on the ACFS website.

| rano | rano | Network observer for AI CLIs |

2. Use category: "utilities" to distinguish from first-class flywheel tools
3. Include appropriate synergies where applicable

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 9 utilities visible on website
2. Proper categorization as utilities
3. Website builds successfully","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:46:44.593801711Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T02:01:14.680436702Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T02:01:14.680364496Z","close_reason":"All 9 utility tools (tru, rano, mdwb, s2p, rust_proxy, aadc, caut, pt, xf) already present in flywheel.ts with full entries","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flywheel","utilities","website"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6h7h","depends_on_id":"bd-1ega","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:10:04.019594635Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6h7h","depends_on_id":"bd-1ega.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:47:15.824609584Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-eh383","title":"meta_skill ARM64 Linux binary missing — blocked upstream (GH #115)","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #115 filed by tayiorbeii (2026-02-06). On ARM64 Linux (aarch64), meta_skill install 404s because v0.1.0 has no aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu binary.

## Available binaries in v0.1.0:
- ms-0.1.0-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.gz (ARM64 macOS - exists)
- ms-0.1.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz (x86_64 Linux - exists)
- ms-0.1.0-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip (Windows - exists)
- aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu — MISSING

## Two-Part Fix

### Part 1: ACFS workaround (THIS repo, can do now)
Update doctor.sh check at lines 1115-1116 to detect aarch64 on Linux and show:
  "meta_skill: ARM64 Linux binary not yet available (see https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/meta_skill/issues/1)"
instead of the generic install suggestion that will 404.

Implementation:
```bash
# In doctor.sh, before the meta_skill install suggestion:
if [[ "$(uname -m)" == "aarch64" ]] && [[ "$(uname -s)" == "Linux" ]]; then
    log_item "warn" "meta_skill (ms)" "ARM64 Linux binary not yet available"
    return 0  # Skip install attempt
fi
```

### Part 2: Upstream (meta_skill repo, blocked)
Add aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu to the GoReleaser or release CI matrix in Dicklesworthstone/meta_skill. Requires:
- Cross-compilation target or dedicated ARM64 runner
- New release (v0.1.1 or v0.2.0)
This is blocked on upstream release work.

## Testing
After Part 1:
1. On this VPS (x86_64): `acfs doctor` should still show meta_skill normally
2. Simulate ARM64: mock uname output or grep the code to verify the aarch64 check exists
3. Verify the issue URL is correct and accessible

## Key Files
- scripts/lib/doctor.sh (lines 1109-1117, meta_skill check)
- Upstream: Dicklesworthstone/meta_skill release CI matrix

## Status
Part 1 is actionable now. Part 2 is blocked upstream. Keep this bead open until both parts are done.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:16:39.015874050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:39:31.599422934Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup#115","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["arm64","bug","meta_skill","upstream-blocked"]}
 {"id":"bd-flfe","title":"Deep exploration: ACFS (Agentic Coding Flywheel Setup)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of ACFS (Agentic Coding Flywheel Setup) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify ACFS installation
[[ -d /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup ]] && echo "PASS: acfs repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: acfs repo missing"; exit 1; }
command -v acfs &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: acfs command available" || { echo "FAIL: acfs not in PATH"; exit 1; }

# Check for manifest
[[ -f /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/acfs.manifest.yaml ]] && echo "INFO: Manifest exists" || echo "WARN: No manifest"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/acfs-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- `cat /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/README.md` - Read full README
- `cat /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/acfs.manifest.yaml` - Check manifest structure

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - Tool installation mechanism
  - Checksum verification (checksums.yaml)
  - Manifest structure and tool definitions
  - Dependency ordering
  - AGENTS.md generation

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
acfs --help 2>&1 | head -20
acfs install --help 2>&1 | head -10
acfs verify --help 2>&1 | head -10

# Test actual functionality
acfs status 2>&1 | head -10
```

### 1.4 Security Verification
```bash
# Check checksums file
cat /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/checksums.yaml | head -20

# Verify checksum mechanism
acfs verify --checksums 2>&1 | head -10 || echo "No verify command"
```

### 1.5 Project State Review
- Check beads in /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/.beads/
- Review recent commits: `cd /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup && git log --oneline -20`

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Tool installation mechanism
- [ ] Checksum verification process
- [ ] Manifest structure (tools, dependencies)
- [ ] AGENTS.md generation
- [ ] Environment setup

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
ACFS is the bootstrap tool - verify it installs these:
- [ ] All flywheel tools (ntm, mail, br, etc.)
- [ ] Dependencies (rust, bun, uv, etc.)
- [ ] Skills and integrations

### 2.3 Manifest Verification
```bash
# Count tools in manifest
grep -c "^  [a-z]" /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/acfs.manifest.yaml 2>/dev/null || echo "Count manually"

# Check dependencies
grep -A5 "depends_on" /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/acfs.manifest.yaml | head -20
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update acfs entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: Bootstrap installer for flywheel
- `description`: Tool installation and verification
- `deepDescription`: How manifest-based installation works
- `features`: Verified capabilities (checksums, AGENTS.md)
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: All tools it installs

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Bootstrap workflow

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES)

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test acfs entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const acfs = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'acfs');
console.log('Testing acfs entry...');
console.assert(acfs, 'acfs entry exists');
console.assert(acfs.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
console.assert(acfs.cliCommands?.length > 0, 'has commands');
console.log('All assertions passed');
"
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Installation Verification
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG=/tmp/acfs-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== ACFS E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG
echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG

# Test 1: Status
echo "Test 1: Status..." | tee -a $LOG
acfs status 2>&1 | head -15 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 2: Verify checksums
echo "Test 2: Verify..." | tee -a $LOG
acfs verify 2>&1 | head -10 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No verify command"

# Test 3: List tools
echo "Test 3: List..." | tee -a $LOG
acfs list 2>&1 | head -10 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No list command"

echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG
echo "Log: $LOG"
```

### 4.4 Content Diff Verification
```bash
echo "=== Changes Made ==="
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true
```

## Phase 5: Completion (FINALIZE)

### 5.1 Rollback Procedure (if tests fail)
```bash
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state"
```

### 5.2 Sync Changes
```bash
br update bd-flfe --status done
br sync --flush-only
```

### 5.3 Final Verification
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [ ] Manifest structure documented correctly
- [ ] Checksum mechanism documented
- [ ] No broken links or references","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:02:56.720155271Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:35:19.069946681Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:35:19.069916263Z","close_reason":"ACFS deep exploration complete: verified v0.5.0, acfs doctor (47+ checks), acfs update (category-specific), acfs cheatsheet (50+ aliases), acfs session. Updated tldr-content.ts.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-gsjqf","title":"Fix update.sh Claude Code channel: bare 'claude update' downgrades to stable (GH #125)","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #125 filed by joyshmitz (2026-02-08). The install path was fixed (commit 4d0174f0 — agents.sh now uses @latest), but update.sh still calls bare "claude update" which forces the stable channel.

## Problem
run_cmd_claude_update() in scripts/lib/update.sh (line 1230) calls bare "claude update" at lines 1254, 1260, 1267, 1269, 1274. This always uses the stable channel. On a SUCCESSFUL update, users get silently downgraded from latest (e.g., 2.1.37) to stable (e.g., 2.1.25). The fallback path (update_run_verified_installer claude latest at line 1157) correctly uses latest, but only fires when "claude update" FAILS.

Additionally, the uca alias in acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc line 158 uses bare "~/.local/bin/claude update" (also stable channel).

## Chosen Fix Approach: Use --channel latest flag
The simplest and most correct fix is to change all `claude update` calls to `claude update --channel latest`. This preserves the native update mechanism (which handles binary replacement, version checking, etc.) while ensuring the correct channel.

We do NOT replace with the verified installer because:
1. `claude update --channel latest` is the officially supported way to update on a specific channel
2. The verified installer is a more heavyweight operation (re-downloads everything)
3. The fallback to verified installer already exists and fires when `claude update` fails

## Specific Changes Required

### 1. scripts/lib/update.sh — run_cmd_claude_update()
- Line 1232: Change cmd_display from "claude update" to "claude update --channel latest"
- Line 1254: `claude update` → `claude update --channel latest`
- Line 1260: `claude update` → `claude update --channel latest`
- Line 1267: `claude update` → `claude update --channel latest`
- Line 1269: `claude update` → `claude update --channel latest`
- Line 1274: `claude update` → `claude update --channel latest`

### 2. acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc — uca alias
- Line 158: Change `alias uca="~/.local/bin/claude update"` to `alias uca="~/.local/bin/claude update --channel latest"`

## Impact
Every user running acfs-update gets silently downgraded from latest to stable. This is actively harmful.

## Key Files
- scripts/lib/update.sh (lines 1229-1296, run_cmd_claude_update())
- scripts/lib/agents.sh (line 21, already fixed to @latest — reference)
- acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc (line 158, uca alias)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:16:19.008573344Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:18:40.313387500Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup#125","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","claude-code","update"],"comments":[{"id":56,"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IMPORTANT: Before implementing, verify that 'claude update --channel latest' is a valid CLI flag. Run 'claude update --help' to check. If --channel does not exist, the fallback approach is: replace the native claude update call with update_run_verified_installer() using 'latest' as the primary path (this function already exists in update.sh and correctly handles the latest channel). Also search for any other aliases that might call bare 'claude update' besides the uca alias (e.g., grep for 'claude update' across all acfs shell configs).","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:59:20Z"},{"id":57,"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BUG IN PROPOSED FIX: The uca alias change oversimplifies. Bead says change alias uca from just claude update to claude update --channel latest. But the ACTUAL alias at line 158 is: alias uca='~/.local/bin/claude update && (bun install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest || bun install -g --trust @openai/codex) && bun install -g --trust @google/gemini-cli@latest'. Following the bead instructions literally would DESTROY the codex and gemini update steps. CORRECT FIX: Change only the claude update portion to claude update --channel latest, preserving the full chain: alias uca='~/.local/bin/claude update --channel latest && (bun install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest || bun install -g --trust @openai/codex) && bun install -g --trust @google/gemini-cli@latest'","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:41Z"},{"id":58,"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CRITICAL: --channel flag DOES NOT EXIST. Running 'claude update --help' shows only -h/--help as options. The proposed fix (claude update --channel latest) will either fail with unknown option or be silently ignored. VERIFIED BY RUNNING: claude update --help => 'Usage: claude update|upgrade [options] Options: -h, --help Display help for command'. THE FALLBACK APPROACH FROM COMMENT 1 IS NOW THE PRIMARY FIX: Replace all 5 bare 'claude update' calls in run_cmd_claude_update() with update_run_verified_installer claude latest (which already exists in update.sh and correctly handles the latest channel at line 1157). For the uca alias, replace '~/.local/bin/claude update' with a function call or inline the verified installer logic. This is a larger change than originally scoped — the function run_cmd_claude_update() essentially becomes a wrapper around update_run_verified_installer. ALTERNATIVE: Check if claude stores channel info in ~/.claude/ config files and whether the update mechanism reads it. If so, we could set the channel config once rather than changing every update call.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:18:19Z"},{"id":59,"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FOLLOW-UP: The round 4 uca alias fix (comment 2) also used --channel latest which does not exist. The corrected alias suggestion (preserving codex/gemini chain) is also wrong in its claude update portion. The uca alias fix depends on the chosen approach for run_cmd_claude_update. If we switch to update_run_verified_installer, the alias would need to call a function or script that wraps update_run_verified_installer, since aliases cannot easily call bash functions defined in update.sh. Alternatively, the uca alias could be changed to invoke the acfs-update command directly (e.g., 'acfs-update --only=claude') if such a flag exists, or to call the official installer inline: alias uca='curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | sh -s -- --channel latest && ...' (need to verify the exact installer URL and flags).","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:18:40Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-gsjqf","title":"Fix update.sh Claude Code channel: bare 'claude update' downgrades to stable (GH #125)","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #125 filed by joyshmitz (2026-02-08). The install path was fixed (commit 4d0174f0 — agents.sh now uses @latest), but update.sh still calls bare "claude update" which forces the stable channel.

## Problem
run_cmd_claude_update() in scripts/lib/update.sh (line 1230) calls bare "claude update" at lines 1254, 1260, 1267, 1269, 1274. This always uses the stable channel. On a SUCCESSFUL update, users get silently downgraded from latest (e.g., 2.1.37) to stable (e.g., 2.1.25). The fallback path (update_run_verified_installer claude latest at line 1157) correctly uses latest, but only fires when "claude update" FAILS.

Additionally, the uca alias in acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc line 158 uses bare "~/.local/bin/claude update" (also stable channel). The full alias chains claude update with codex and gemini updates: alias uca='~/.local/bin/claude update && (bun install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest || bun install -g --trust @openai/codex) && bun install -g --trust @google/gemini-cli@latest'

## CRITICAL: --channel flag DOES NOT EXIST
Verified by running: claude update --help => only -h/--help available. The originally proposed fix (claude update --channel latest) is INVALID. This was confirmed by running the actual command on this VPS.

## Chosen Fix: Replace run_cmd_claude_update() with verified installer
The update_run_verified_installer function already exists at update.sh:715-737 and correctly handles the latest channel. It:
1. Looks up the installer URL from KNOWN_INSTALLERS[claude] = "https://claude.ai/install.sh" (security.sh:183)
2. Fetches the installer script
3. Verifies its SHA-256 checksum against checksums.yaml
4. Pipes to bash with args (e.g., "latest")
The fallback path at line 1157 already uses this: run_cmd "Claude Code (reinstall)" update_run_verified_installer claude latest

The fix replaces the entire run_cmd_claude_update() function body with a call to update_run_verified_installer, preserving the logging/error-handling wrapper while switching the underlying mechanism from bare "claude update" to the verified installer with the "latest" argument.

For the uca alias, replace the "~/.local/bin/claude update" portion with an inline verified-installer invocation, OR redirect through a wrapper script, OR call the update function. The alias must preserve the codex and gemini update chain.

## Affected Files
- scripts/lib/update.sh (lines 1229-1296, run_cmd_claude_update(); lines 1150-1161, caller)
- acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc (line 158, uca alias)
- scripts/lib/security.sh (line 183, KNOWN_INSTALLERS[claude] — reference only)
- scripts/lib/agents.sh (line 21, CLAUDE_PACKAGE — reference only; line 148, verified installer call — reference for pattern)

## Sub-tasks (see child beads)
1. Audit: scan for ALL bare "claude update" occurrences (bd-gsjqf.1)
2. Rewrite run_cmd_claude_update() to use verified installer (bd-gsjqf.2)
3. Fix uca alias and any other occurrences found in audit (bd-gsjqf.3)
4. Comprehensive testing (bd-gsjqf.4)
5. End-to-end verification and GH #125 close (bd-gsjqf.5)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:16:19.008573344Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:24:46.965782404Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup#125","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","claude-code","update"],"comments":[{"id":56,"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IMPORTANT: Before implementing, verify that 'claude update --channel latest' is a valid CLI flag. Run 'claude update --help' to check. If --channel does not exist, the fallback approach is: replace the native claude update call with update_run_verified_installer() using 'latest' as the primary path (this function already exists in update.sh and correctly handles the latest channel). Also search for any other aliases that might call bare 'claude update' besides the uca alias (e.g., grep for 'claude update' across all acfs shell configs).","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:59:20Z"},{"id":57,"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BUG IN PROPOSED FIX: The uca alias change oversimplifies. Bead says change alias uca from just claude update to claude update --channel latest. But the ACTUAL alias at line 158 is: alias uca='~/.local/bin/claude update && (bun install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest || bun install -g --trust @openai/codex) && bun install -g --trust @google/gemini-cli@latest'. Following the bead instructions literally would DESTROY the codex and gemini update steps. CORRECT FIX: Change only the claude update portion to claude update --channel latest, preserving the full chain: alias uca='~/.local/bin/claude update --channel latest && (bun install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest || bun install -g --trust @openai/codex) && bun install -g --trust @google/gemini-cli@latest'","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:41Z"},{"id":58,"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CRITICAL: --channel flag DOES NOT EXIST. THE FALLBACK APPROACH FROM COMMENT 1 IS NOW THE PRIMARY FIX: Replace all 5 bare 'claude update' calls in run_cmd_claude_update() with update_run_verified_installer claude latest (which already exists in update.sh and correctly handles the latest channel at line 1157). For the uca alias, replace '~/.local/bin/claude update' with a function call or inline the verified installer logic. The corrected alias suggestion (preserving codex/gemini chain) is also wrong in its claude update portion. The uca alias fix depends on the chosen approach for run_cmd_claude_update. If we switch to update_run_verified_installer, the alias would need to call a function or script that wraps update_run_verified_installer, since aliases cannot easily call bash functions defined in update.sh. Alternatively, the uca alias could be changed to invoke the acfs-update command directly (e.g., 'acfs-update --only=claude') if such a flag exists, or to call the official installer inline: alias uca='curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | sh -s -- --channel latest && ...' (need to verify the exact installer URL and flags).","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:18:40Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-gsjqf.1","title":"Audit: scan all files for bare "claude update" occurrences","description":"## What
Scan the entire ACFS codebase for every occurrence of bare "claude update" (without --channel or equivalent). This determines the full scope of the fix before any code changes.

## Why (P1 — scope discovery)
We know about 5 occurrences in update.sh and 1 in acfs.zshrc, but there could be others in documentation, other shell configs, helper scripts, or CI workflows. Missing even one occurrence means users can still get silently downgraded.

## Scan Commands
```bash
# Primary search: all shell scripts
grep -rn "claude update" scripts/ acfs/ --include="*.sh" --include="*.zsh" --include="*.zshrc" --include="*.bashrc"

# Broader search: all files (might find docs, CI, configs)
grep -rn "claude update" . --exclude-dir=.git --exclude-dir=node_modules --exclude-dir=.beads

# Specifically check for the alias pattern
grep -rn "uca\|claude update" acfs/
```

## Expected Findings (from prior analysis)
1. scripts/lib/update.sh:1254 — `claude update 2>&1 | tee -a ...`
2. scripts/lib/update.sh:1260 — `claude update >> ...`
3. scripts/lib/update.sh:1267 — `claude update || exit_code=$?`

 {"id":"bd-jt48","title":"Deep exploration: BR (beads_rust)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of BR (beads_rust) and revise its description with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification

```bash
#!/bin/bash
LOG=/tmp/br-preflight.log
echo "=== BR Pre-flight ===" | tee $LOG

[[ -d /dp/beads_rust ]] && echo "PASS: Directory exists" || exit 1
command -v br &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: br installed: $(which br)" || echo "WARN: br not in PATH"
br --version 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG

SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/br-exploration-snapshots
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
```

## Phase 1: Research

### 1.1 Documentation
```bash
cat /dp/beads_rust/README.md
cat /dp/beads_rust/AGENTS.md 2>/dev/null
```

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- JSONL schema: examine actual .beads/issues.jsonl format
- Dependency system: blocks/blocked_by implementation
- Auto-flush mechanism: when/how it triggers
- bd alias: how it works
- CLI commands: full inventory

### 1.3 Verify CLI Commands
```bash
#!/bin/bash
echo "=== BR CLI Verification ===" | tee /tmp/br-cli.log

br --help 2>&1 | tee -a /tmp/br-cli.log
br create --help 2>&1 && echo "PASS: br create" || echo "FAIL"
br update --help 2>&1 && echo "PASS: br update" || echo "FAIL"
br show --help 2>&1 && echo "PASS: br show" || echo "FAIL"
br list --help 2>&1 && echo "PASS: br list" || echo "FAIL"
br sync --help 2>&1 && echo "PASS: br sync" || echo "FAIL"
br ready 2>&1 && echo "PASS: br ready" || echo "FAIL"
br blocked 2>&1 && echo "PASS: br blocked" || echo "FAIL"

# Test bd alias
command -v bd &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: bd alias exists" || echo "WARN: bd alias missing"
```

### 1.4 Verify JSONL Schema
```bash
# Examine actual schema from a real bead
head -1 /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/.beads/issues.jsonl | jq . 2>/dev/null | tee /tmp/br-schema.json
```

### 1.5 External Context
```bash
/xf search 'beads_rust OR br cli OR issue tracking' 2>&1 | head -30
cass search 'br beads issue' --robot --limit 10 2>&1
```

## Phase 2: Analysis

Document with VERIFICATION:
- [ ] JSONL schema fields: [list actual fields from schema]
- [ ] CLI commands VERIFIED: create, update, show, list, sync, ready, blocked
- [ ] bd alias: VERIFIED working ✓/✗
- [ ] Auto-flush: trigger conditions verified
- [ ] Tech stack: Rust VERIFIED
- [ ] Synergies VERIFIED:
  - [ ] bv: bv reads br data ✓/✗
  - [ ] mail: any integration ✓/✗
  - [ ] ntm: any integration ✓/✗

## Phase 3: Revision

Update with VERIFIED content:
- List only commands that actually work
- Document actual JSONL schema fields
- Only list verified synergies

## Phase 4: Testing

```bash
#!/bin/bash
cd /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web

bun run type-check 2>&1 | tee /tmp/br-typecheck.log
bun run lint 2>&1 | tee /tmp/br-lint.log
bun run build 2>&1 | tee /tmp/br-build.log

lsof -t -i :3000 | xargs kill 2>/dev/null; sleep 2
bun run dev &
sleep 10
curl -sL http://localhost:3000/flywheel | grep -qi 'br' && echo "PASS" || echo "FAIL"
curl -sL http://localhost:3000/tldr | grep -qi 'br' && echo "PASS" || echo "FAIL"
kill %1

RESULTS=/tmp/br-exploration-test-results.log
cat /tmp/br-cli.log > $RESULTS
grep -E "PASS|FAIL" /tmp/br-*.log >> $RESULTS
```

## Phase 5: Commit

```bash
[[ $(grep -c "FAIL" /tmp/br-exploration-test-results.log) -gt 0 ]] && exit 1

git add apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
git commit -m "docs(flywheel): update BR with verified CLI commands and schema

- Verified all CLI commands work
- Documented actual JSONL schema
- Verified bd alias
- Tested auto-flush behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>"

br update bd-jt48 --status closed
br sync --flush-only && git add .beads/ && git push
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CLI commands VERIFIED
- [ ] JSONL schema documented
- [ ] bd alias VERIFIED
- [ ] All tests PASS
- [ ] Pushed
